task_completion.description: InVesalius 3 application is open with the CT Cranium dataset loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 GUI is visible, displaying medical imaging views (axial, coronal, sagittal) of a skull/brain scan.

task_completion.description: The window and level settings are adjusted to a 'Brain' preset or manually set to WW ≈ 80 and WL ≈ 40.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The UI indicates Window Width (W or WW) around 80 and Window Level (L or WL) around 40. The scan itself should show distinct mid-gray brain tissue, distinguishing it from stark black/white bone window settings.

task_completion.description: The axial view is navigated to a slice where the lateral ventricles are clearly visible (slice index between 40 and 80).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice indicator for the axial view shows a number between 40 and 80. The axial image displays the characteristic dark, fluid-filled, butterfly or horn-like shapes of the lateral ventricles in the center of the brain.

task_completion.description: The axial view is exported as a PNG to /home/ga/Documents/brain_window_slice.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Picture' or 'Save As' dialog box in InVesalius showing the file name 'brain_window_slice.png' being saved to the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager/terminal showing the file has been created at that path.

task_completion.description: The selected axial slice number is recorded as an integer in /home/ga/Documents/slice_info.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, or terminal echo command) displaying the file 'slice_info.txt' in the Documents folder, containing a single integer that matches the slice number navigated to in InVesalius.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually used the InVesalius 3 GUI to adjust settings and navigate, rather than using a background script to extract DICOM data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the InVesalius interface (e.g., clicking menus, adjusting window/level sliders, scrolling through slices).

integrity.description: Verifies the image was exported using the application's built-in tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of InVesalius export dialogs or menu interactions (like clicking an export camera icon or File -> Export Picture), rather than terminal commands using external tools like ImageMagick or Python to convert DICOMs to PNG.

integrity.description: Verifies the recorded slice number matches the actual slice shown in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The integer written in slice_info.txt exactly matches the slice index visible in the InVesalius axial view during the export step.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CT Cranium dataset contains 108 total slices. The anatomically correct slice range for viewing the lateral ventricles is between slice 40 and 80. The standard 'Brain' window setting is Window Width (WW) 80 and Window Level (WL) 40, which produces a mid-gray appearance for brain tissue (unlike bone windows which are mostly stark black and white). The expected output paths are exactly /home/ga/Documents/brain_window_slice.png and /home/ga/Documents/slice_info.txt.
